item.title: Texas agency to set rules for using treated fracking wastewater on farmland
item.description: The state’s environmental regulator will hold a public hearing June 15 on its proposed rules for how the toxic wastewater can be reused. Here’s what you should know.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Texas takes over voter registration in Val Verde County amid struggles
item.dc:creator: Natalia Contreras

item.title: Paxton’s office threatens legal action if Big 12 penalizes Texas Tech for playing QB who placed bets
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Corpus Christi’s delayed water response bubbles up to Texas governor’s race
item.description: Frustrated by a lack of progress, Gov. Greg Abbott threatened state action. Gina Hinojosa, his Democratic opponent, accused him of backing industry over people.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: James Talarico launches Spanish-language World Cup TV ad
item.description: The more than $800,000 ad buy — the first by either candidate in the general election — continues the Austin Democrat’s push to win Latino voters as his race against GOP nominee Ken Paxton ramps up.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Screwworm was found in a dog in Texas. What does that mean for your pets?
item.description: Experts say not to panic. These are the signs to look for to protect your fur babies.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Elected Officials Directory
item.description: To help Texans know more about their representatives, we’ve gathered the bios, contact information and more for the occupants of the state’s highest offices.

item.title: Government Salaries Explorer
item.description: Review the compensation of hundreds of thousands of state and municipal employees in Texas.

item.title: Texas Public Schools Explorer
item.description: Review enrollment trends, demographics, teacher experience, pay and outcomes for all schools, and more.
